title: replace tuple-vec chat_entries with named struct
description: In update(), lines 144-164 collect self.state.chats into a Vec<(String, String, bool)> to break the borrow before click handling mutates self.state. The three-tuple loses field names at the for-loop call site: (chat_id, label, is_selected) must be read in order with no guard against transposition. Suggested rewrite: define a small struct ChatEntry { id: String, label: String, is_selected: bool } and collect into Vec<ChatEntry>. This costs zero runtime overhead, self-documents the tuple slots, and makes future additions (e.g. unread_count: u32) non-breaking positionally.
